channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Outside26 jeffreys st nw1, 10th March
item.description:
A new Street lights have been put in our streets the one outside 26 Jeffrey’s st has stopped working a month ago it’s only been put in two months ago. It’s very dark and dangerous in that part of the street.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Jeffrey's Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Manhole cover surrounds need filling

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensway, Enfield

item.title: Huge sofa, 10th March
item.description:
Huge sofas an chairs

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Appleby Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Flytipped bike by the side of the path, 10th March
item.description:
Flytipped bike by the side of the path. At 51.378, 0.067 according to image metadata, which is more accurate than the position on the map, which does not show the footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bramley Close, Locksbottom, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 10th March
item.description:
Lots of rubbish and not enough bins. Reported by Liberal Democrat Stockwell team.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1a, Lorn Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flags on lamposts all over Erith Spreading anti ethnic minority messagaing, 10th March
item.description:
According to a YouGov opinion poll conducted between 3 and 16 October 2025, "Both white and ethnic minority adults tend to see this summer’s lamppost flag raisings as primarily about spreading an anti-migrant or anti-ethnic minority message, rather than a display of patriotism". The results also showed that "Ethnic minority adults tend to say they would be uncomfortable if large numbers of England flags were raised in their local area in such a manner".[100] 50% of 3,248 adults surveyed by YouGov thought that those attaching England flags to lampposts were doing so mostly as a way of expressing anti-ethnic minority or anti-migrant sentiment, 25% thought they were doing so mostly as an expression of national pride, and 25% thought both motivations were equal. Amongst ethnic minority adults, the figures were 55%, 15% and 20% respectively. When the question was asked about the Union Flag instead, 39% (and 41% of ethnic minority respondents) saw it as an expression of anti-ethnic minority or anti-migrant sentiment, 30% (26% of ethnic minority respondents) as an expression of national pride, and 24% (23% of ethnic minority respondents) saw it as both equally. 52% of ethnic minority and 36% of white adults responded that they saw the England flag as a racist symbol, but only 26% of ethnic minority and 19% of white adults thought the same of the Union Flag.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bronze Age Way, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road failure again, 10th March
item.description:
Road has been repair numerous times and has started cracking and breaking up across the road from 272/274. The volume, speed and size of vehicles tramping up and down this road is causing untold damage from vibrations to my property and other homes on this stretch of Burn Oak Lane. In addition to this the last large area of repair left a more uneven road surface which resulted in water pooling at the front of my property. This can take days to subside! This whole stretch of road needs reviewing and a plan formulated to resolve these issues properly, not just remedial action to tick a box. Please arrange to revisit this problem. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 272, Burnt Oak Lane, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog waste needs clearing near Southdown House, 10th March
item.description:
Dog waste near steps by the playground

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albrighton Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattresses dumped near Southdown House, 10th March
item.description:
Mattresses dumped without any indication of collection. Need removing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albrighton Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars parked in double yellow blips blocking drive, 10th March
item.description:
This happens almost every night between 8 and 10pm. Meaning myself and 3 other garage owners cannot our car in or out.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement cover flap broken on Bedale Street, 10th March
item.description:
Pavement cover flap is broken so there is a hole in the ground which is a trip hazard. Please can this be fixed?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2-4, Bedale Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very uneven pavement with slabs sticking out at different directions, 10th March
item.description:
I tripped over today and grazed my knee. I also broke my phone and ripped my jacket.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Union Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Escalated missing collection, 10th March
item.description:
Since June 2025, our bin collection has been stopped. We have to call and report it each time, and it takes weeks to be collected. The last collection was on 24th February and the bins are full. Also, delivery drivers and people who stop for shopping throw their food rubbish around. We have been tidying around the bins, but none of this is our duty. We need the rubbish to be collected immediately and the floor to be cleaned, as this has not been caused by us. Secondly we need cctv on that area. Those people who stop on that area pee around.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Widmore Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 80cm x 145 cm x 15cm Pothole on Goffers Road SE3, 10th March
item.description:
The pothole is located approximately 30 meters from the junction of Shooter's Hill and Goffers Rd SE3 7BN on the left hand side of the road when heading towards Blackheath Village. The map coordinates are 51º 28' 17.0" N, 0º 00' 00.2" E. It is an unlit road at this point and the pothole was sufficiently deep to have destroyed my tyre.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Goffers Road, Blackheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and bags of rubbish dumped in between shrubs behind phone box on Rodway Road, 10th March
item.description:
In shrubs between phone box on Rodway Road to entrance tomorrow Hawes Road, there is rubbish everywhere, including black bags of household waste. Please can you send a team to clear up, there is a lot of rubbish in road and in between the shrubs. Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rodway Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: It is right outside my house, 10th March
item.description:
This rubbish has not been cleaned up by the tenants , they live next door to me and I have a baby living here and I am now paranoid I’m going to ants and mice in my home which I like to keep clean

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Philimore Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti all over, 10th March
item.description:
Graffiti all over the forest and paths.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidbrooke Park Road, Kidbrooke

item.title: Light not working at all ( street light behind phone box on Rodway Road), 10th March
item.description:
Please fix light, not working at all. It’s the light behind the phone box on Rodway Road (behind sinclaircottages).
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Horsley Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 6 old cars parked around no 49 Goddard Rd, 10th March
item.description:
6 old cars parked around 49 Goddard Rd, the home of the owner, query if they are taxed or insured as they rarely move, cause parking problems.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Goddard Road, Elmers End, Greater London
